events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a production welding task on a manufacturing floor. The observed condition is "spatter/cleanliness reject." The work is rough-complete. AWS D1.1/D1.3 and shop WPS are referenced.

Spatter and cleanliness issues in welding can lead to rejection under AWS D1.1/D1.3 if they interfere with inspection or serviceability. Excessive spatter and poor cleanliness are non-compliant conditions. Since it's a "reject," the decision should be "fail."

Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant.

Risk: medium (spatter/cleanliness reject is a quality issue requiring rework, not necessarily safety-critical immediately, but could affect weld quality).

Percent complete: rough-complete, maybe 80%.

Findings: excessive weld spatter, inadequate cleaning of weld area, surface contamination.

Actions: clean weld area, remove spatter, re-inspect per AWS D1.1/D1.3 and WPS.

References: AWS D1.1/D1.3, shop WPS.
